01 ·

About Me

I am a statistics graduate from the University of Ibadan, Nigeria, graduating as the Best Graduating Student with a CGPA of 3.79/4.00 and First Class Honours. My work sits at the intersection of Bayesian inference, machine learning, and applied quantitative modelling.

My undergraduate thesis developed the Convoluted Bayesian Reference Class Forecasting framework — explicitly modelling uncertainty across overlapping reference classes via Gaussian mixture modelling, closed-form posterior derivation, and Bayesian model averaging for cancer incidence prediction.

I am particularly motivated to combine rigorous statistical reasoning with modern AI architectures to build scalable, high-impact solutions for health analytics, disaster risk modelling, and financial risk assessment — especially in data-scarce sub-Saharan African settings.

Beyond research, I have mentored and tutored hundreds of students across secondary and undergraduate levels in mathematics and statistics, and held multiple leadership positions at the University of Ibadan.

Bayesian Statistics Spatiotemporal Modelling Survival Analysis Machine Learning Epidemiological Analytics Forecasting Econometrics Financial Risk

02 ·

Research & Publications

2026
Preprint
Flexible Hazard Modelling in Intensive Care Survival Analysis: A Bayesian Generalized Log-Logistic AFT Approach

Proposed and implemented a Bayesian GLLD-AFT regression model to address limitations of conventional parametric models in capturing unimodal and non-monotone hazard functions in ICU data. Published as a preprint on ResearchGate.

Publication Bayesian Methods Survival Analysis ICU Analytics
2025
Thesis
Efficiency of Convoluted Bayesian Reference Class Forecasting: Statistical Properties and Applications

Developed an original forecasting framework explicitly modelling uncertainty across multiple overlapping reference classes. Applied Gaussian mixture modelling for latent class discovery, closed-form Bayesian posteriors, and model averaging to predict U.S. state-level cancer incidence. Evaluated predictive accuracy and Bayes risk against OLS and standard RCF benchmarks. Supervised by Dr. A.T. Adeniran. Under preparation for journal submission.

Manuscript in Prep Forecasting Bayesian Inference Reference Class
2025
Ongoing
Integrating Spatiotemporal Bayesian Models and Mobility Data for Infectious Disease Forecasting in Sub-Saharan Africa

Ongoing research aimed at forecasting infectious disease spread in data-scarce settings using spatiotemporal Bayesian approaches integrated with human mobility data.

Ongoing Spatiotemporal Epidemiology Sub-Saharan Africa
2025
Project
Spatiotemporal Disaster Prediction using ConvLSTM & Graph Neural Networks

Modelled spatial dependencies and temporal disaster dynamics using graph-based deep learning architectures. Supervised by Dr. T.P. Ogundunmade.

Deep Learning ConvLSTM GNN Disaster Risk
2024
Project
ICU Vital Sign Forecasting using LSTM and Transformer Models

Implemented LSTM and Transformer architectures for early detection of critical patient deterioration from ICU vital sign time series. Supervised by Mr. T.O. Omooseti.

LSTM Transformers Time Series Healthcare AI
2024
Project
Loan Default Prediction using Ensemble Methods

Applied XGBoost and Random Forest to model determinants of credit risk and evaluate predictive accuracy for loan default prediction. Supervised by Dr. O.J. Akintande.

XGBoost Random Forest Credit Risk FinTech

Experience

Feb 2025 – Present
Data Science Intern
Codveda Technologies · Remote
  • Web scraping & data collection with Python, BeautifulSoup and pandas; handled pagination, dynamic content, structured datasets (CSV/JSON).
  • Built and evaluated predictive models: regression, classification (Logistic Regression, Random Forest, SVM), and unsupervised learning (K-Means) using scikit-learn.

  • NLP text classification using NLTK and TF-IDF for sentiment analysis and spam detection.

UI-LISA · University of Ibadan (On-Site)
